components.title: Shared theme wrapper
components.lead: This site-level block renders before the page-local examples.
paragraphs: Use the shared layout wrapper to verify how global sections look in the workshop theme.

components.title: Shared theme footer
paragraphs: This repeated site-level footer confirms the theme can frame page content without duplicating JSON on every page.

metadata.description: Single-page preview of every component rendered with the workshop theme.

components.headline: Workshop theme preview
components.subheadline: Tactile surfaces, serif-led headings, and craft-forward accents on one review page.

components.title: Theme navigation sample
components.lead: This compact control confirms route pickers stay readable in the active theme.

items.title: Fast visual QA
items.body: Use this page to inspect how spacing, type, and CTA treatments land in the workshop theme.

items.title: Component coverage
items.body: Each supported component appears once so theme regressions are easier to spot.

items.title: Stable content
items.body: The copy stays intentionally synthetic so visual differences are easier to attribute.

items.title: Heading tone
items.body: Compare how the same hierarchy feels when typography shifts.

items.title: Surface treatment
items.body: Cards, dividers, and section framing should reflect the active theme tokens.

items.title: CTA contrast
items.body: Primary and secondary buttons should stay readable without per-page CSS.

items.title: Long-form rhythm
items.body: Prose, FAQs, and captions should stay balanced on the same page.

items.question: Why does each theme only get one page?
items.answer: The goal is quick comparison, so one page with full component coverage is enough.

items.question: Can these examples use placeholder content?
items.answer: Yes. They are intended for layout and theme review, not for publishing.

items.question: Should the examples be built together?
items.answer: Yes. The bulk examples build generates every theme preview in one deterministic run.

components.headline: Build every theme example at once
components.body: Run the bulk examples build to regenerate the full preview set into dist/examples.

components.title: Shared sample map
components.caption: The map component stays in the preview so iframe framing and spacing are visible in every theme.

components.alt: Sample workspace image used for the workshop theme preview
components.caption: Media is included with a caption so image framing and supporting text can be checked together.

components.title: Sample contact form
components.intro: Theme previews also include a form so field spacing and button treatment stay covered.
components.deliveryNote: This is a preview-only form block used for theme validation.

components.title: How to use this page
components.lead: Scroll once and check every section against the same baseline content.
paragraphs: The preview fixtures are intentionally synthetic. They exist to make theme and component comparisons obvious without depending on customer content.
Because each theme file follows the same structure, visual changes are easier to attribute to theme tokens rather than content differences.
